title: Hypotheses: si + imparfait → conditionnel
description: Talk about unreal or unlikely situations with the si-clause pattern: si + imparfait in one half, conditionnel présent in the other.

cards.notes: Same stem as the futur simple, but the imparfait endings: *je parlerais, tu parlerais, il parlerait…*

cards.back: the hypothesis pattern: si-clause in the imparfait, result in the conditional
cards.notes: *Si j'avais le temps, je voyagerais.* — If I had time, I would travel. Never put the conditional in the *si*-clause.

cards.back: If I had time (imparfait of avoir)
cards.notes: The *si*-clause uses the **imparfait** (*avais*), not the conditional.

cards.front: être → serais; avoir → aurais; faire → ferais; aller → irais
cards.back: irregular conditional stems (same as the future)
cards.notes: The conditional borrows the **future stem**, so the same irregulars apply: *serais, aurais, ferais, irais, viendrais, pourrais, voudrais*.

cards.back: I would like a coffee. (polite conditional)
cards.notes: The conditional also softens requests: *je voudrais*, *pourriez-vous…?*

cards.front: Si tu venais, je serais content.
cards.back: If you came, I would be happy.
cards.notes: imparfait (venais) in the si-clause, conditional (serais) in the result.

steps.title: Forming the conditional
steps.body:
# The conditional présent

The conditional says what **would** happen. It is easy to build:

> **future stem + imparfait endings** (-ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient)

| | parler | finir |
|---|---|---|
| je | parlerais | finirais |
| tu | parlerais | finirais |
| il/elle | parlerait | finirait |
| nous | parlerions | finirions |
| vous | parleriez | finiriez |
| ils | parleraient | finiraient |

**Irregular stems** are the same as the futur simple: *être → ser-*, *avoir → aur-*, *faire → fer-*, *aller → ir-*, *venir → viendr-*, *pouvoir → pourr-*, *vouloir → voudr-*.

The conditional is also the polite form: *Je **voudrais** un café.*

steps.title: The si-clause pattern
steps.body:
# Hypotheses with si

For an unreal or unlikely present/future situation, use this pattern:

> **Si + imparfait**, **conditionnel présent**

- *Si j'**avais** le temps, je **voyagerais**.* — If I had time, I would travel.
- *Si tu **venais**, je **serais** content.* — If you came, I would be happy.

## The golden rule

**Never** put the conditional in the *si*-clause. The *si*-clause takes the **imparfait**; the conditional goes in the **other** half.

- ✅ Si j'avais… je ferais…
- ❌ Si j'aurais… (wrong!)

Either half can come first: *Je voyagerais si j'avais le temps.*

exercise.prompt: Match each verb with its je-conditional form.
Fill in the imparfait of avoir: "If I had time, I would travel."
exercise.sentence: Si j'___ le temps, je voyagerais.
exercise.hint: si-clause = imparfait, not conditional. avoir → avais.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the conditional of être: "If you came, I would be happy."
Translate: *If I were rich, I would travel.* (être → imparfait; voyager → conditional)
distractors: Si je serais riche, je voyagerais.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the polite request: *I would like a coffee, please.*
